result.stdout:
Description:
  The Xchange Connector CLI

Usage:
  xchange [command] [options] [[--] <additional arguments>...]]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help                                                   Show help and usage information
  --version                                                        Show version information
  -v, --verbosity <Debug|Error|Fatal|Information|Verbose|Warning>  Controls the verbosity of logging. [default: Information]

Commands:
  client       Manage clients for the Connector source.
  code         Manage code submissions for the Connector source.
  connector    Manage the Connector source.
  login        Log into Xchange platform using TrimbleID.
  logout       Logout of the Xchange platform.
  module       Manage modules in the Connector source.
  data-object  Manage data objects in the Connector source.
  action       Manage action handlers in the Connector source.
  test         Manage Connector testing.
  extract      Extract the Connector metadata from the Connector source.

Additional Arguments:
  Arguments passed to the application that is being run.

commands.payload:
Description:
  The Xchange Connector CLI

Usage:
  xchange [command] [options] [[--] <additional arguments>...]]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help                                                   Show help and usage information
  --version                                                        Show version information
  -v, --verbosity <Debug|Error|Fatal|Information|Verbose|Warning>  Controls the verbosity of logging. [default: Information]

Commands:
  client       Manage clients for the Connector source.
  code         Manage code submissions for the Connector source.
  connector    Manage the Connector source.
  login        Log into Xchange platform using TrimbleID.
  logout       Logout of the Xchange platform.
  module       Manage modules in the Connector source.
  data-object  Manage data objects in the Connector source.
  action       Manage action handlers in the Connector source.
  test         Manage Connector testing.
  extract      Extract the Connector metadata from the Connector source.

Additional Arguments:
  Arguments passed to the application that is being run.

result.stdout:
Description:
  Creates a new action handler.

Usage:
  xchange action new [options] [[--] <additional arguments>...]]

Options:
  --connector-path                                                 The path of the folder in which the connector project lives. Defaults to the current working directory if not set. [default: /tmp/inspectra-static-trimble.xchange.connector.cli-1.4.12-6ce369bde05e4b64b9dc8141e9b57853]
  --module-id (REQUIRED)                                           The module id of the module to which a new action handler will be added. Must exist in the connector.json file.
  -o, --object-name (REQUIRED)                                     The name of the data object to create an action handler for. PascalCase is preferred.
  -n, --action-name, --name (REQUIRED)                             The name of the action to create an action handler for. PascalCase is preferred.
  --client-name                                                    The name of the API client class to use in the action templates. This is typically auto-detected and does not need to be specified.
  -?, -h, --help                                                   Show help and usage information
  -v, --verbosity <Debug|Error|Fatal|Information|Verbose|Warning>  Controls the verbosity of logging. [default: Information]

Additional Arguments:
  Arguments passed to the application that is being run.

result.stdout:
Description:
  Creates a client for interfacing with application.

Usage:
  xchange client new [options] [[--] <additional arguments>...]]

Options:
  --connector-path                                                                                            The path of the folder in which the connector project lives. Defaults to the current working directory if not set. [default: /tmp/inspectra-static-trimble.xchange.connector.cli-1.4.12-6ce369bde05e4b64b9dc8141e9b57853]
  -t, --type <Http|Unknown>                                                                                   Type of client to create. HTTP is used by default. [default: Http]
  --auth, --auth-type <ApiKey|Basic|Custom|OAuth2ClientCredentials|OAuth2CodeFlow|OAuth2Password> (REQUIRED)  Type of authentication to use.
  --api-client-name, --client-name, --name                                                                    Name of the client to create. 'ApiClient' by default. [default: ApiClient]
  -?, -h, --help                                                                                              Show help and usage information
  -v, --verbosity <Debug|Error|Fatal|Information|Verbose|Warning>                                             Controls the verbosity of logging. [default: Information]

Additional Arguments:
  Arguments passed to the application that is being run.

result.stdout:
Description:
  Manage code submissions for the Connector source.

Usage:
  xchange code [command] [options] [[--] <additional arguments>...]]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help                                                   Show help and usage information
  -v, --verbosity <Debug|Error|Fatal|Information|Verbose|Warning>  Controls the verbosity of logging. [default: Information]

Commands:
  submit    Archives the connector code and creates a submission for approval.
  get       Looks up a code submission for the provided correlation ID.
  status    Looks up a code submission status for the provided correlation ID.
  list      Looks up all code submissions for the Connector.
  close     Closes the open code submission for the provided correlation ID.
  feedback  Retrieve an archive containing feedback of the code submission for the provided correlation ID.

Additional Arguments:
  Arguments passed to the application that is being run.

result.stdout:
Description:
  Looks up all code submissions for the Connector.

Usage:
  xchange code list [options] [[--] <additional arguments>...]]

Options:
  --connector-path                                                                                                                                                The path of the folder in which the connector project lives. Defaults to the current working directory if not set. [default: /tmp/inspectra-static-trimble.xchange.connector.cli-1.4.12-6ce369bde05e4b64b9dc8141e9b57853]
  --api-key, --token                                                                                                                                              Token for calling Xchange API.
  --submitter                                                                                                                                                     Get submissions for submitter.
  --getOpen                                                                                                                                                       Get all open submissions.
  --getFinished                                                                                                                                                   Get all finished submissions.
  --status <Approved|Building|Closed|ClosedDraft|Halted|OpenDraft|PendingApproval|PendingPublish|PlatformError|Processing|Published|Publishing|Rejected|Unknown>  Get all submissions for status.
  --limit                                                                                                                                                         Amount of submissions to return. [default: 10]
  -?, -h, --help                                                                                                                                                  Show help and usage information
  -v, --verbosity <Debug|Error|Fatal|Information|Verbose|Warning>                                                                                                 Controls the verbosity of logging. [default: Information]

Additional Arguments:
  Arguments passed to the application that is being run.

result.stdout:
Description:
  Archives the connector code and creates a submission for approval.

Usage:
  xchange code submit [options] [[--] <additional arguments>...]]

Options:
  --connector-path                                                 The path of the folder in which the connector project lives. Defaults to the current working directory if not set. [default: /tmp/inspectra-static-trimble.xchange.connector.cli-1.4.12-6ce369bde05e4b64b9dc8141e9b57853]
  --api-key, --token                                               Token for calling Xchange API.
  --override-archive                                               Override code archive if it already exists.
  --submitter                                                      The person who is submitting the code. Defaults to the email address of the logged in user. Not needed if you are signed in.
  --dry-run                                                        Test and validate your submission before sending to Xchange.
  --draft                                                          Sets the submission as a draft to allow for early feedback on progress of the Connector.
  --allow-breaking-changes                                         Allow breaking changes in the connector.json when sending to Xchange
  -?, -h, --help                                                   Show help and usage information
  -v, --verbosity <Debug|Error|Fatal|Information|Verbose|Warning>  Controls the verbosity of logging. [default: Information]

Additional Arguments:
  Arguments passed to the application that is being run.

  --override-archive                                               Override code archive if it already exists.
  --submitter                                                      The person who is submitting the code. Defaults to the email address of the logged in user. Not needed if you are signed in.
  --dry-run                                                        Test and validate your submission before sending to Xchange.
  --draft                                                          Sets the submission as a draft to allow for early feedback on progress of the Connector.
  --allow-breaking-changes                                         Allow breaking changes in the connector.json when sending to Xchange
  -?, -h, --help                                                   Show help and usage information
  -v, --verbosity <Debug|Error|Fatal|Information|Verbose|Warning>  Controls the verbosity of logging. [default: Information]

  Create a new Connector project

Usage:
  xchange connector new [options] [[--] <additional arguments>...]]

  -n, --name (REQUIRED)                                            Name of Connector.
  connector-key, --key                                             Key for Connector.
  -d, --description                                                Description of Connector.
  --allow-prerelease                                               Allows usage of pre-release versions of the Connector SDK packages if one is available greater than latest stable. [default: False]
  -?, -h, --help                                                   Show help and usage information
  -v, --verbosity <Debug|Error|Fatal|Information|Verbose|Warning>  Controls the verbosity of logging. [default: Information]

Additional Arguments:
  Arguments passed to the application that is being run.

  Creates a new data object

Usage:
  xchange data-object new [options] [[--] <additional arguments>...]]

Options:
  --connector-path                                                 The path of the folder in which the connector project lives. Defaults to the current working directory if not set. [default: /tmp/inspectra-static-trimble.xchange.connector.cli-1.4.12-6ce369bde05e4b64b9dc8141e9b57853]
  --module-id (REQUIRED)                                           The module id of the module to which a new data reader will be added. Must exist in the connector.json file.
  -n, --name, --object-name (REQUIRED)                             The name of the data object to create data reader for. PascalCase is preferred.
  -t, --reader-type <Full|Incremental>                             The name of the data reader for the Data Object. (Options are: full, incremental) [default: Full]
  --no-pagination                                                  Skips pagination from the generated data reader. [default: False]
  --client-name                                                    The name of the API client class to use in the action templates. This is typically auto-detected and does not need to be specified.
  -?, -h, --help                                                   Show help and usage information
  -v, --verbosity <Debug|Error|Fatal|Information|Verbose|Warning>  Controls the verbosity of logging. [default: Information]

Additional Arguments:
  Arguments passed to the application that is being run.

  --output-file-name, --output-filename                            JSON file name that will contain the extracted Connector metadata. [default: connector.json]

result.stdout:
Description:
  Creates a new module in the referenced connector.

Usage:
  xchange module new [options] [[--] <additional arguments>...]]

Options:
  --connector-path                                                 The path of the folder in which the connector project lives. Defaults to the current working directory if not set. [default: /tmp/inspectra-static-trimble.xchange.connector.cli-1.4.12-6ce369bde05e4b64b9dc8141e9b57853]
  --id, --module-id (REQUIRED)                                     The id of the module to which a module will be created. Must not already exist in the settings.json file.
  -n, --name (REQUIRED)                                            Name of the module to add. (e.g. "Project Management")
  --key, --module-key, --url-part (REQUIRED)                       The key defining the module in our platform. Must not already exist in the settings.json
  --version (REQUIRED)                                             The version of the module.
  -d, --description                                                A description of what the module is for.
  --add-health-checks                                              Add health check endpoints to the module. [default: False]
  -?, -h, --help                                                   Show help and usage information
  -v, --verbosity <Debug|Error|Fatal|Information|Verbose|Warning>  Controls the verbosity of logging. [default: Information]

Additional Arguments:
  Arguments passed to the application that is being run.

Description:
  Manage Connector testing.

Usage:
  xchange test [command] [options] [[--] <additional arguments>...]]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help                                                   Show help and usage information
  -v, --verbosity <Debug|Error|Fatal|Information|Verbose|Warning>  Controls the verbosity of logging. [default: Information]

Commands:
  init   Initializes test data so that a developer can easily set the flow data they want to use in test.
  reset  Resets local testing databases.

Additional Arguments:
  Arguments passed to the application that is being run.
